methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public companies in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proved applicable. This approach uses the $26M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement in the sector, yielding $65M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow biotech filter (only 2 same-stage peers identified, below the required minimum of 3). Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 6.1x P/S median across three public tickers was inapplicable because Amber Bio's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, centered at $65M. This range reflects modest downside and upside variance around the stage-progression midpoint. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data precluding multiple-based valuation, a thin peer set limiting cross-validation, and typical seed-stage execution risk. Additional financial disclosure or comparable transaction data would strengthen conviction.
